sessions.lever: explored 4 dup-read forms to defeat CSE on the D_80101ED6 read; none beat floor 10

adjudicator deep-dive: 5 additional forms beyond session 1; v9 (arm-local s32 v1 + duplicated func_800494D4 call INSIDE each arm) reached masked floor 13 = baseline+3 — closest form ever recorded. Cross-jump merging works (addu/addu/lbu/jal tail matches target exactly, operand order included). Residual gap is RA cascade: v9 source triggers `move s2,s0` in jal func_800493E4 delay slot, else-arm references s2 (0x67c, 0x688), adding a 2nd live copy of s0, growing save set + frame -32→-40. Target keeps single s0 live across both arms — RA decision not source-derivable from levers tried.
sessions.lever_rule: split-read-defeats-hoist v9 = arm-local-var + per-arm-call-duplication; structural match achieved, RA cascade residual

sessions.rationale: Owner ruling: 'park permanently' is not a disposition. Canonical-asm signal is WEAK (scan_hand_coded LOW score 0, no STRONG signals). Cycle in blocked until STRONG hand-coded signal or pure-C match. Next worker: resume from v9, attack the RA cascade (move s2,s0 in the jal func_800493E4 delay slot).

reviewer.evidence: No candidate adopted (no floor improvement). HEAD's 12 regfix rules inject the duplicated reads; no new cheat introduced. The only pure-C path to the duplication is the FORBIDDEN volatile coercion.

remaining_gap.summary: Target duplicates the identical-symbol read *(s16*)(&D_80101ED6 + s0) into each if/else arm (result in v1) with a shared 'addu v0,v0,a0; addu v0,v0,v1; lbu' tail. Pure-C GCC CSE-hoists that identical memory read to ONE shared load after the merge, changing both branch shapes + the tail (10 masked diffs incl. a 1-insn operand-order diff). The split-read-defeats-hoist dup lever does not apply: both arms read the SAME global, so there is no symbol-known arm to index directly; CSE re-merges regardless. Only volatile (forbidden) would force the re-read.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 12 regfix rules (4 delete + 6 insert + 1 subst) that delete the shared hoisted load and inject duplicated lui/addu/lh %hi/%lo(D_80101ED6) loads into each branch arm.

next_hypotheses: Resume from v9: arm-local `s32 v1` + duplicate the func_800494D4 call INSIDE each if/else arm. Cross-jump merges the addu/addu/lbu/jal tail correctly; structural match is achieved. Floor 13 = baseline+3.

Attack the RA cascade: v9 triggers `move s2,s0` in the jal func_800493E4 delay slot, growing the callee-save set. Target avoids it. Try (a) reorder the s0-using statements so GCC schedules a different delay-slot filler, (b) introduce a no-spill structural change that keeps s0 live across both arms without a copy, (c) examine what the s2 copy is feeding and whether a different sequencing eliminates the second live range.
Last-resort permuter run seeded from v9 candidate.c targeting only the s2/s0 RA decision (other 60 insns are stable).
